page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1707.19, while multi-core performance reaches 6818.25. Memory bandwidth is notable, with cached read-only speeds at 462.32 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 44.16 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression tasks show strong results, with gzip compression reaching 30.32 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.4 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the poor range at 754.28, despite hardware AES support.

The c8gb.xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile optimized for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The absence of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated ARMv9 cores provide predictable compute density without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. This configuration is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves 10.11 GB/sec throughput, Redis in-memory key-value stores processing 9.23 million SET operations per second, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores built on the arm64 ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, a 36 MB L3 cache, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps but lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show average CPU and memory capabilities, with strong single-threaded compression speeds, such as 30.32 GB/sec in gzip. However, cryptographic performance is weak, as indicated by a poor Passmark encryption score. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this server is designed for network-reliant, compute-focused workloads. It is highly suitable for static web serving, Redis database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose text or image processing tasks where a 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio is optimal.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized arm64 server with 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for web serving and Redis workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.xlarge is a compute-optimized arm64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 compute-optimized instance delivering balanced memory bandwidth and strong single-threaded compression for network-reliant workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 36 MB L3 cache
Delivers 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong single-threaded compression performance with 30.32 GB/sec in gzip
Best suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
